pages.raw_text:
The annual banquet of the
chamber of commerce last night at
the Silver Grill brought out a big
and enthusiastic attendance. Di-
rectors ere elected as follows for
the coming year:


For the three year term, T. H.
Brewer, F. E. Goodall, Charles
Hebbard, Sam Glasgow and A. l..
White; for two years, D. T. Ham,
l.. Mclean, F. r. Culbertson, H. l..
Blocker and E. V. Cartier Van
Dissel; for the one year term, c.
NJ Fassett, w. S. McCrea, j. d.
Sherwood, F. B. Wright and w. E.
Goodspeed.


The banquet was served in eight
courses OF almost exclusively home
grown products. At the close a
business session followed in which
the officers made their annual re-
ports.


BY unanimous vote members
went on record in favor of paying
congressman $4,000 per year.


The speech program included 16
toasts, Mayor Daggett being the
first speaker. He advocated civil
service in municipal affairs.


Jay P. Graves was given an en-
thusiastic reception. He explained
his plans for the building of cleo.
trio lines connecting Spokane with
the great agricultural districts of
the Inland empire. He ridiculed
the arguments being advanced
against granting the franchise for
the subway, explaining that the
tunnel is intended to connect pas
senger and freight terminals and
that constant use of steam would
ruin the electrical power apparatus
He also said hat the subway
would provide terminals for the
Milwaukee, North Coast and Ket
tie River roads if built to this city
trains to be hauled through the
subway by electric motors.

subway" said Mr.. Graves. ito serve
